status.integration_note: This isolated art package does not alter compiled room records, existing graybox asset bindings, content/runtime hashes, collision, interaction ranges, room activation, Bargain offers, reward commits, extraction, or terminal authority.

scope.layout: layout.core_private_life_01 fixed B0 -> B1 -> B2 -> B3 -> B4 -> B5 -> B6 chain
scope.purpose: Player-visible candidate landmarks for the three non-combat state pivots that are currently only tilemap/graybox record surfaces: B0 safe vestibule arrival, B4 rest/Bargain anchor, and B6 post-reward stable exit.
out_of_scope: asset registration or replacement of existing asset records
room layout, encounter, reward, safety, bargain, boss, or extraction behavior
code, task documentation, README, tests, commit, and push

authority.current_task: docs/tasks/GB-M03-03G.md#Current Next Step; fixed-room combat, rewards/pending inventory, terminal producers, and ordinary route remain open.

selection_rationale: The current candidates cover the microrealm route entrance, repeated Bell enemy silhouettes, initial hostile VFX, and pending-loot risk. The fixed M03 layout still has three distinct player-facing noncombat pivots with no dedicated runtime art candidate: safe B0, the B4 rest/Bargain shrine anchor, and the stable B6 exit that may exist only after committed boss reward. This route-state landmark set is therefore more immediate and less duplicative than a new terminal/successor surface, which already has death portrait/font material and cannot truthfully advance before terminal authority is live.

authored_record_alignment.B0: CONT-ROOM-007 classifies B0 as the safe vestibule outside the six-room count. The ward is a visual arrival marker only; it cannot grant safety, readiness, admission, or room transition.
authored_record_alignment.B4: CONT-ROOM-007 requires B4 as the rest room and core records bind its shrine anchor. The three suspended talismans are a visual review motif for the three-choice Bargain surface; they do not choose, reveal, validate, or apply any Bargain.
authored_record_alignment.B6: CONT-BOSS-001 and the Caldus record create the stable exit only after committed boss reward. The open gate candidate must never be shown before that authority and cannot prove reward/extraction state itself.
authored_record_alignment.existing_asset_boundary: The current `sprite.portal.exit.dungeon.bell_sepulcher` remains an existing graybox marker. The B6 image is a review candidate for a later explicit binding, not a registration or replacement.

reference_image.role: visual-language reference only: charcoal stone, tarnished brass, and pixel density

generation.source_provenance: All raw chroma sources were generated during this task and copied unchanged from the default generated-images directory to source/. No third-party asset was used.
generation.alpha_postprocess: C:/Users/micha/.codex/skills/.system/imagegen/scripts/remove_chroma_key.py --auto-key border --soft-matte --transparent-threshold 12 --opaque-threshold 220 --despill. Auto-sampled source key colors: vestibule #0af70c; Bargain shrine #0df20f; exit #0af80d.
generation.normalization: Pillow RGBA crop-to-alpha bounds, nearest-neighbor scale into a maximum 176px content box, center horizontally, and bottom-align to a transparent 192x192 runtime canvas.
generation.preview_process: Original and 2x sheets use an opaque checkerboard only to display alpha; runtime files retain transparent corners.

prompts.B0_vestibule: Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: M03 Bell Sepulcher B0 safe-vestibule landmark candidate, single static transparent-runtime-ready sprite. Input image: existing Bell Sepulcher entrance candidate, used only for dark stone, tarnished brass, and pixel-density visual language. Primary request: one in-dungeon safe vestibule marker: squat waist-high ash-stone plinth topped by one hooded lantern and small iron ward plate. It reads as quiet B0 dungeon arrival/ready point before combat, not hostile spawn, loot, exit portal, shrine, merchant, or healing zone. Scene/backdrop: perfectly flat #00ff00 chroma-key background, no floor/shadow/reflection/gradient/texture. Style: top-down three-quarter 2D dark-fantasy pixel-art-like landmark; wet charcoal stone, tarnished brass, ash, muted warm lantern light, crisp nearest-neighbor clusters. Centered square, bottom-center anchor, readable 96-192px. No text/letters/numbers/UI/arrows/characters/weapons/skulls/treasure/chest/coin/violet magic/red hostile effect/green/blue/map pin/doorway/portal swirl/cross/safe-zone circle/cast shadow/watermark/#00ff00. No combat telegraph or prompt; hard crisp silhouette, no blur.
prompts.B4_bargain_shrine: Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: M03 Bell Sepulcher B4 rest/Bargain-room shrine landmark candidate, single static transparent-runtime-ready sprite. Input image: existing Bell Sepulcher entrance candidate, used only for dark stone, tarnished brass, and pixel-density visual language. Primary request: optional Veil Bargain shrine for safe noncombat B4: low cracked ash-stone altar, small suspended dark iron censer, exactly three dim violet glass talismans in a compact triangle. It reads as optional risky choice/rest landmark, not enemy, attack telegraph, healing well, loot chest, exit, merchant, or ordinary safe arrival. Flat #00ff00 chroma background. Style: wet charcoal stone, tarnished iron/brass, ash, restrained muted violet only on three hanging talismans, crisp clusters. Centered square, bottom anchor, readable 96-192px. No text/letters/numbers/UI/arrows/characters/weapons/skulls/treasure/chest/coin/lantern/cross/bright gold/cyan/green/red hostile effect/safe-zone ring/portal swirl/shadow/watermark/#00ff00. Exactly three talismans; no fourth offering; no combat telegraph/prompt; hard crisp silhouette/no blur.
prompts.B6_post_reward_exit: Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: M03 Bell Sepulcher B6 post-reward stable-exit landmark candidate, single static transparent-runtime-ready sprite. Input image: existing Bell Sepulcher entrance candidate, used only for dark stone, tarnished brass, and pixel-density visual language. Primary request: compact stable dungeon-exit interactable appearing only after committed Caldus reward: open narrow stone sepulcher gate, broken iron chain across threshold, one small tarnished-brass bell over lintel, calm pale ash-white passage light beyond open bars. It reads successful return path, not dungeon entrance, unsafe combat portal, loot chest, merchant, healing well, hostile magic, or generic safe-zone. Flat #00ff00 chroma background. Style: wet charcoal stone, tarnished brass, iron, ash-white interior light only, crisp clusters. Centered square, bottom anchor, readable 96-192px. No text/letters/numbers/UI/arrows/characters/weapons/skulls/treasure/chest/coin/lantern/cross/violet magic/red hostile effect/green/cyan/gold glow/safe-zone ring/portal swirl/shadow/watermark/#00ff00. Broken chain visibly separate from open gate; no combat telegraph/prompt; hard crisp silhouette/no blur.

preview_files.note: Original-scale checkerboard review: B0 vestibule ward, B4 Bargain shrine, B6 post-reward exit, left to right.

review_gates: B0 review: the warm ward must read as a safe vestibule landmark without implying a mechanical safety grant, heal, portal, or interactable range.

B4 review: three visible talismans must communicate optional Bargain choice without looking like three selected offers, enemy projectiles, or a safe exit; the actual Bargain panel remains authoritative.
B6 review: only display after the committed reward/exit authority; distinguish it from the closed microrealm dungeon entrance and reject any pre-commit presentation.
Route review: inspect B0/B4/B6 at 1280x720 and 1920x1080 in standard and reduced-effects modes alongside gameplay HUD/telegraphs; preserve readable playfield and collision/anchor alignment.
Integration review: explicit renderer and record binding, collision/range validation, interaction prompt, room activation, reward receipt, and terminal evidence are all still required.
license: Generated for this workspace during this task. Retain raw sources, exact prompts, and hashes with any future integration.
